questions.question: At what pitch and bank angles do the flight director command bars disappear from the PFD?

options.text: Pitch exceeding +20/-15 degrees or bank exceeding 45 degrees
Pitch exceeding +30/-20 degrees or bank exceeding 65 degrees
Pitch exceeding +25/-25 degrees or bank exceeding 60 degrees
They never disappear — they remain visible at all attitudes

questions.explanation: The command bars are removed from the PFD if pitch exceeds +30/-20 degrees or bank exceeds 65 degrees. At those extreme attitudes, the flight director cannot provide useful guidance.

questions.question: At what bank angle does ESP automatically disconnect itself?

options.text: 45 degrees
60 degrees
30 degrees
75 degrees

questions.explanation: Exceeding 75 degrees of bank automatically disconnects ESP entirely. This prevents ESP from interfering if the pilot needs full control authority during an unusual attitude recovery.

questions.question: How do you override ESP during training maneuvers like steep turns?

options.text: Turn ESP off through the G1000 menu system
Press and hold the CWS or Autopilot Disconnect button throughout the maneuver
Pull the ESP circuit breaker before beginning the maneuver
ESP automatically deactivates when the autopilot is off

questions.explanation: ESP can be overridden by pressing and holding the CWS (Control Wheel Steering) button or the Autopilot Disconnect button. Either must be held throughout the entire maneuver — releasing re-enables ESP.

questions.question: Below what altitude AGL is the Underspeed Protection System NOT available?

options.text: 500 feet AGL
1,200 feet AGL
200 feet AGL
100 feet AGL

questions.explanation: Minimum speed protection is not available within 200 feet AGL. At low altitude, the pilot is responsible for maintaining adequate airspeed.

questions.question: During an AFCS go-around, what must the pilot do that the autopilot does NOT handle?

options.text: Retract the landing gear
Select the missed approach course on the G1000
Manually increase the power levers
Press the NAV button to track the missed approach

questions.explanation: During an AFCS go-around, the autopilot handles pitch and roll guidance via the flight director, but the pilot must manually increase the power levers. Throttle management is always the pilot's responsibility.
